label: Data Abstraction
description: The process of hiding irrelevant details from the user. It shows only necessary information to the user and hiding all the irrelevant details from the user.

label: Data Independence
description: Users and data should not directly interact with each other.

label: Database Management System (DBMS)
description: A system where data abstraction will work.

label: Levels of Data Abstraction
description: Total three levels in database management system: View level, conceptual level and physical level.

label: Physical Level
description: The lowest level of data abstraction. It defines how data are stored, decides location in memory, and whether data will be centralized or distributed.

label: Conceptual Level
description: Defines what data are stored into the database and what are the relationships exist among this data. It decides the structure of entire database.

label: View Level
description: Tells the application about how data should be shown to the user. There are multiple views of the same database.

label: Database Administrator (DBA)
description: Decides which data should be kept at which particular disk drive. Handles physical level, conceptual level and logical level.

label: Irrelevant Details
description: Details hidden from the user by data abstraction, such as where data will be physically stored or what types of data model will be used.

label: Necessary Information
description: Essential information that users know, not the irrelevant details.

label: User
description: Present on the view level. Users are not directly interact with the physical level.

label: Database
description: Directly connected to the physical level. Physical data will be stored at physical level.

label: Programmer/Developer
description: Handle conceptual level and logical level.

label: Database Structure
description: The structure of entire database, including tables and their columns, and relationships between tables, decided in the logical level.

label: Database Queries
description: Different types of queries like Select, Insert, Update, Delete performed in logical level.

label: Front End System
description: Term used for View level.

label: Back End System
description: Term used for Physical level.

label: Security
description: Increases in the view level because authority for editing, changes, and modification is given to specific roles.

label: Server
description: Is a logical level. Fetches the answer of information through the database.

label: 3 Tier Architecture
description: An architecture seen in the last presentation.

label: View of Data
description: The focus of this presentation.

label: Multiple Views
description: Views existing at the presentation level; reasons for needing them are revealed in this presentation.

label: Database Goal
description: To store and retrieve data conveniently and effectively.

label: Data Storage
description: Involved for storing and retrieving data.

label: Storage Data Structures
description: May involve simple or complex data structures for data storage.

label: Complexity
description: Information to be hidden by data abstraction.

label: Essential Details
description: Provided when hiding complexity.

label: Abstract Data
description: A short and sweet answer; desired over complete data.

label: Complete Data
description: Not desired; involves encountered complexities.

label: Logical Level
description: Deals with what data are actually stored and the relationship among data stored.

label: Low Level Data Structures
description: Used at the physical level, especially for multimedia data.

label: What Data Stored
description: Describes the data actually to be stored in the databases.

label: Data Relationships
description: Describes the relationship among the data stored in the database.

label: Simple Data Structures
description: Used at the logical level.

label: Physical Data Independence
description: Exists so that if any changes are made in the logical level, it will not affect the physical level.

label: Application Level
description: Where the logical level is, and applications reside.

label: Schema
description: The structure of the database.

label: Database Administrator
description: Involved in all levels; writes all the logic, especially at the logical level.

label: User Access
description: Granting privileges to users, managed at the view level.

label: User-System Interaction
description: Done at the view level through interfaces.

label: Interfaces
description: Web, mobile, GUI; used for user interaction with the system.

label: Security of Multiple Views
description: An important aspect to focus on at the view level.
